title: Canonical Patch
description: Normalized schema for canonical-patch artifacts in multi-user-turn-codebench. Covers both layers: data-pipeline/artifacts_<source>/canonical_patches/<sid>.json (source) and harbor_tasks/<task>/reference_patch.json (reference). Both layers share this single schema; layer-specific differences are expressed via the `_layer` discriminator. Stub records (no canonical patch available) are valid by setting `_status: "no_canonical"` and omitting the patch fields.

name_status_line.description: git diff --name-status line: status code + tab + path (and renames have a second tab + new path).

numstat_line.description: git diff --numstat line: additions + tab + deletions + tab + path. Binary files are '-\t-\tpath'.

schema_version.description: Bump on any breaking change to this schema.

_task_name.description: Harbor task directory name. Stable join key across layers.

_source.description: Upstream session corpus. NOT a method label; do not collapse with `_extraction.method`.

_status.description: `canonical` = patch fields are present and meaningful. `no_canonical` = stub; patch fields MUST be omitted or empty and `_category` MUST be set.

_category.description: Required iff `_status == "no_canonical"`. Reason the patch could not be recovered.

session_id.description: Upstream session UUID. Null for upstream-PR-derived patches.

_base_commit.description: Commit BEFORE the fix that the Docker environment clones. Replaces SWE-bench `base_commit`.

_repo_url.description: Full https:// URL. Use this; do NOT derive from `repo_id`.

patch.description: Unified diff in `git diff` format (the canonical fix). Equivalent to SWE-bench `patch`. UTF-8. Required when `_status == "canonical"`.

patch_truncated.description: Always written. True iff the patch was hit by an upstream rollout truncation cap (Hyperswitch 1000-char), in which case `_fidelity` MUST be `lossy` or `directional`.

_fidelity.description: Top-level fidelity tier — how much can downstream trust this as a textual gold patch? Canonical values: `exact` = byte-equivalent replay/upstream diff; `high` = minor formatter/whitespace drift but content-correct; `directional` = right files + intent match, content partially reconstructed; `lossy` = structurally incomplete; `verifier_aligned` = curator hand-tuned to satisfy `tests/test.sh`; `no_canonical` = stub (paired with `_status: no_canonical`). Transitional legacy values still carried by a handful of curator-tagged records pending re-bucketing: `verified` (triton-windows-rebase-buildhelpers and 1 hand_curated); `clean`, `reconstructed`, `upstream_canonical` (2 records each, restored from the curator's original tag rather than collapsed into `high`/`exact`). New writes SHOULD pick from the canonical 6. Mirrored by `_extraction.fidelity` for the same record (the nested field is the per-extraction-step view; this top-level field is the consumer-facing summary).

files_changed.description: `git diff --name-status` output. One line per file; each line matches `name_status_line`. Trailing newline allowed. MUST NOT be numstat-shaped.

numstat.description: `git diff --numstat` output. Canonical shape is an array of numstat-line strings (one entry per file). String form is accepted for legacy reads — newline-joined `git diff --numstat` text where each line matches `numstat_line`.

files_changed_count.description: MUST equal the non-blank line count of `files_changed`.

commit_message.description: Commit-message-like description. For reconstructed patches use the literal string `[reconstructed from session messages]` for explicitness.

commit_sha.description: SHA of the upstream resolving commit when available. Null for reconstructed patches with no upstream linkage.

is_agent_author.description: Whether the original session was driven by an AI agent (as opposed to a human commit).

method.description: Single canonical vocabulary (matches the normalized corpus). Collapses the 19+ legacy spellings — `tool_replay`/`session_message_curation` → `messages_replay`; `github_pr_diff`/`upstream_pr` → `gh_pr_diff`; `upstream_commit` → `upstream_commit_diff`; all `hand_*` variants → `hand_curated` with details in `note`.

fidelity.description: Per-extraction-step view of fidelity. Same vocabulary as top-level `_fidelity` except `none` is allowed here (paired with `method: none` for stubs) and `no_canonical` is not — for stubs use `none` at this level and `no_canonical` at the top level. `verified` is a transitional legacy label (see top-level `_fidelity`).

verified.description: Has someone manually compared this against the upstream artifact and signed off?

source.description: Free-form provenance string (e.g. file path, gh URL).

status.description: Trust signal. `RESOLVED` was a historical post-fix transient state and now collapses to `RELIABLE`.

_review.description: Latest review pass. Older reviews (round1, pre-promote) move into `_review_history`.

_review_history.description: Append-only audit trail. Replaces the proliferation of `_review_pre_promote`, `_triple_check`, `_triple_check_round2`, `_triple_check_pre_promote`, `_triple_check_round2_pre_promote`, `_review_fix`, `_review_pre_remediation`.

_curation.description: Optional manual-curation metadata. Replaces the 9 scattered `_curation_*` and `_candidate_*` keys.

_install_config_commit_sha.description: Commit recorded by the harness's install_config.json when synthesizing the buggy state. Lets us detect drift if the Dockerfile is bumped.

_canonical_source_path.description: Reference-layer only. Relative path back to the authoritative source-layer JSON. Required when `_layer == "reference"`.

_sync_note.description: Reference-layer only. Free-form note from the sync script (e.g. `sync_reference_patches.py`).

_n_mutating_ops.description: Number of mutating tool calls (Edit/Write/MultiEdit/Bash-with-redirect) found in the session. 0 implies `_status == "no_canonical"` with `_category == "no_mutating_ops"`.

FAIL_TO_PASS.description: Aspirational SWE-bench-style gate list. Currently empty across the corpus; reserved for the next migration that pulls this from test_manifest.yaml.

PASS_TO_PASS.description: Aspirational SWE-bench-style regression gate list. Currently empty across the corpus.
